title: Make DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS immutable (tuple or Final)
analysis: DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS in dashboard/src/dashboard/config.py:14 is currently a mutable list: `DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS = ['http://localhost:8002']`. The task requires converting it to a tuple with a Final annotation for immutability. The only consumer in production code is the dataclass default_factory on line 24 which already does `list(DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS)` — this works identically on tuples since list() accepts any iterable. In tests, line 10 of test_scaffold.py pins the value against a list literal `['http://localhost:8002']` which must be updated to a tuple literal. Lines 19 and 52 use `list(DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS)` and need no changes. Final is not used anywhere else in the project; this will be the first usage, imported from typing (compatible with the existing `from __future__ import annotations`).

steps.description: Add a failing test `test_default_fused_memory_urls_is_immutable` in TestConfigDefaults that asserts `isinstance(DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS, tuple)`. This test will fail because the constant is currently a list.

In config.py: (1) add `from typing import Final` to imports, (2) change line 14 from `DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS = ['http://localhost:8002']` to `DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS: Final = ('http://localhost:8002',)`. In test_scaffold.py: update line 10 assertion from `== ['http://localhost:8002']` to `== ('http://localhost:8002',)` so the existing pinned-value test matches the new tuple type. Lines 19 and 52 (`list(DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS)`) need no changes.

design_decisions.decision: Use tuple + Final rather than just Final alone
design_decisions.rationale: Final only prevents reassignment at the type-checker level — it does not prevent mutation of a list's contents at runtime. A tuple provides actual runtime immutability. Combining both gives both runtime safety and static analysis protection.

design_decisions.decision: Import Final from typing, not typing_extensions
design_decisions.rationale: Final has been available in typing since Python 3.8. The project uses from __future__ import annotations already, so there are no forward-compatibility concerns.

design_decisions.decision: Keep list(DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS) in the dataclass default_factory unchanged
design_decisions.rationale: list() accepts any iterable including tuples, so the defensive copy pattern still works. The fused_memory_urls field should remain a mutable list since consumers may need to modify their local copy.

design_decisions.decision: Update pinned-value test to compare against tuple literal, not use list() wrapper
design_decisions.rationale: The test on line 10 is specifically testing the constant's value AND type. Comparing against a tuple literal `('http://localhost:8002',)` is more explicit and catches type regressions.

reuse.what: Existing defensive list() copy pattern in default_factory
reuse.how: No change needed — list() already works on tuples, so the existing pattern is reused as-is
These assertions compare cfg.fused_memory_urls (a list) against list(DEFAULT_FUSED_MEMORY_URLS) — works identically on tuples, no changes needed
